steps.title: Substitute x = 12 to find m
steps.content:
Given $j(x) = mx + 144$, and $j(12)=18$. Substitute these values into the function:
$$18=m\cdot 12+144$$
Solve this equation for $m$ to find the slope of the linear function.
This step uses direct substitution with known points.

steps.title: Calculate the value of m
steps.content:
From $$18 = 12m + 144$$,
subtract 144 from both sides to isolate terms with $m$:
$$18 - 144 = 12m\quad \Rightarrow \quad -126 = 12m.$$
Divide both sides by 12 to solve for $m$:
Simplifying and solving gives us the slope.

$$m=\frac{-126}{12}=-\frac{42}{4}=-\frac{21}{2}.$$

steps.title: Find j(10) with the calculated slope
steps.content:
With $$j(x)=-\frac{21}{2}x+144,$$ find $j(10)$:
$$j(10) = -\frac{21}{2}\cdot 10 + 144.
Plug in x=10 after finding m.
  • Calculate the product: $$-\frac{21}{2} \times 10 = -105.$$
  • Add 144 to -105:
$j(10)=-105+144=39.$
